rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Immediately upon the occurrence of facts making it necessary or appropriate to increase the amount of the bond, the personal representative, or the guardian or conservator of the estate, must make an ex parte application for an order increasing the bond. An accounting will not be approved until the correct amount of bond has been ordered and proof of additional bond has been filed. [CRC 7.204(a) or (b).] (2) The amount of income stated in paragraph 15 of the report does not appear to be correct. The accounting, for a period of less than one full year, shows receipts totaling $316,040.21. (3) Additional fees will not be granted for an interim accounting, only for a final accounting. (4) The account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] (5) Any difference between the statement(s) and the account must be reconciled. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Cash in the Merrill Lynch account as of 12/3/22 differs from the balances shown in the statement ending 12/30/22. (Only beginning and ending statements are required to be filed. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).] (6) Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under CRC 2.257. [LRSF 14.60 A.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No Notice of Conservatee's Death appears to have been filed in the conservatorship case. [Probate Code Section 2361.] It is unnecessary to summarize all of the prior orders approving prior accountings unless an order directly affected the current accounting or petition. Transfers of cash between accounts are not changes in the form of assets. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).])

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ROWAN STRUMMER TELLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice may not be mailed, served, or published before the filing of the pleading requiring notice.[LRSF 14.14 A.] The "supplement" that amended the accounting was not filed until 8/8/23. Notice was reportedly mailed on 8/7/23. (2) An AMENDED accounting was attached to this purported "supplement". [CRC 7.3.] Pleading captions should accurately identify the nature of the pleading. (The fact that the second accounting was also amended by erroneously captioned "supplements" led to the incorrect assets on hand at the end of second accounting being assumed to be the correct assets on hand at the beginning of this accounting.) (3) Pages in the amended accounting are still in random order so that page 2 of 2 of Schedule A (receipts) does not immediately follow page 1 of 2 of Schedule A, but follows page 2 of 2 of the Non-Cash Assets on Hand at the Beginning of Account Period, which itself, illogically, still follows Schedule F (changes in the form of assets) instead of following immediately behind Cash Assets on Hand at Beginning of Account Period. The AMENDED accounting should have put the schedules in the proper order. (4) In any event, the subtotals of the two pages of receipts do not total $768.16. (5) Schedule C (disbursements) and unspecified Schedule __ (other credits) still do not include a full description of all entries, such as the DATE OF ANY ORDER permitting reimbursements or what the reimbursements were for or who the ORIGINAL PAYEE was. (6) To WHOM did Miriam Telles pay $433 and $437 on 12/21/22 for "tax payment" and $684 for "bond pmt"? (7) What is the AAA Annual Fee for? (8) Why were there IRS and FTB tax payments made twice in 2022? (9) What do the various losses included on the unspecified schedule represent? Which assets incurred the losses? Were the Market Square LLCs liquidated? Please file the LLC papers substantiating these losses or verifying their sales/liquidations. (10) The number of shares of the newly acquired securities is missing from both Schedule E (non-cash assets on hand at the end of the account) and in paragraph 10 of the supplement. Paragraph 10 of the supplement also does not include the purchase prices for these new assets. (11) The market values of these securities acquired on 3/3/22 are the same on 1/31/23 as their carry values? (12) If the guardian will be reimbursing the minor's father's estate for fees she erroneously paid out of that estate, there must be a liabilities schedule in this accounting. (The order for final distribution of that estate was filed in 2018.) A FULLY AMENDED ACCOUNTING PETITION must be filed. It should stand on its own and fully amend and restate the current petition that was filed on 3/7/23 and not be filed as a question-and-answer form. Notice must be served after the amended accounting petition is filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTHIE MAE BISHOP
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues to be cleared: 1. Need information of the trustee of the Pooled Special Needs Trust for Donald. Also, notice to be given to the trustee. 2. Need information of the trustee of the Jamie Bishop Special Needs Trust. Also, notice to be given to the trustee. 3. Need allegation why the cash distribution to the minor heir should not be deposited into blocked account until the minor reaches majority instead of deposit into UGMA account with custodian. LRSF 14.35.E. 4. Need to show funds are being deposited in compliance with FDIC limit. Prob. Code 9700. 5. Need clarification if Donald still receives mails at the address listed as notice was sent to the real property sold in Nov. 2022. 6. 13100 affidavit of James Bishop is deficient - #7 missing James' share 1/25 despite his signature. 7. Reimbursement of mileage $3,166.80 and tolls $488.80 to be absorbed by statutory fees. LRSF 14.57.B. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally (in Dept 210) or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 742 2364 and password 053549. The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(c) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIAN YATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The I&A showed the address of the real property as 688 York Street. Did the probate referee appraise the correct property? No Corrected I&A has been filed to show the address as 1688 York Street. If the probate referee appraised the wrong property, the hearing might need to be continued further for the correct property to be appraised. (2) The prayer is for final distribution of the real property (without the legal description, as required by CRC 7.651(b)) "and any remaining assets of the Estate" to the three heirs (the surviving spouse as to 50% and each parent as to 25%), but the proposed order includes an order distributing community property. If the administrator wishes the Court to order any distribution of community property, she must amend her petition to seek that relief. Notice of an amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. Or the other interested parties could waive notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/13/23. (Only one copy of each filed paper needs to be provided.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. Why "account" when there is nothing to account for? When attaching an exhibit with a SSN, the SSN should be redacted.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD J. GIETZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/24/23 due to failure to follow this Court's procedures regarding ex parte petitions and CRC 7.103(c). LRSF 14.11 B.: Presentation of Ex Parte Applications or Petitions Not Requiring an Appearance ("Drop-off"). 1) Generally: For ex parte petitions not requiring a personal appearance, a courtesy copy of the petition and the proposed order must be left with the file clerk at the Probate window in Room 103 if the petition is filed over the counter. If the petition is E-filed, the filing party must promptly deliver the courtesy copy and proposed order to Room 202. ... The Probate Department adopts LRSF 2.7 B. with the following clarification and exception: 1. A "courtesy copy" is an endorsed-filed, single-sided copy. [CRC 2.102 and 2.117.] [LRSF 14.7 B.] ... Unless otherwise specified, Examiners and Investigators will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/23. (No earlier availability.) 1) Attachment 8 should have included the full heirship chart, but at the very least, the decedent's parents and their dates of death should have been set forth. 2) Closest living heirs is not definitive. If a first cousin predeceased, his or her issue must be identified and must receive notice. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 3) Both maternal and paternal sides must be represented. Who were Decedent's mother's grandparents and her cousins or more remote cousins, be they Decedent's first cousins once removed or second cousins...? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/3/23. A very poor copy of the will (not a true and correct copy) was attached to this amended petition; the main dispositive provision is barely legible. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DISHUI LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Decedent's father Benchi Liu's Petition: Continued to 11/15/23. (No earlier availability.) (1) The attorney for this petitioner represented the initial petitioner, the decedent's former wife, whose petition has not yet been dismissed or withdrawn. Are the parties fully aware of, and do they knowingly waive, the conflict and their right to new/different counsel? (Merely substituting out as counsel for one petitioner does not eliminate the conflict. [California Rules of Professional Conduct 1.7.]) (2) The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no phone number). (3) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (4) If the estate is estimated at $200,000, and the heirs at law have not (cannot) waive bond, the full amount of the estimated estate must be bonded. (The mother is not an heir when a decedent is survived by issue.) (5) It appears that a guardian ad litem should first be appointed for the minor. This may be done via an ex parte petition. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The third paragraph of Attachment 3g(2)(a) was unnecessary and not authority in any event.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIN HSIN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 6 of the petition must be completed if no issue survived the decedent. (2) The copy of the petition received by the examiner does not appear to be a true and complete copy; it is neither signed nor verified. (3) A copy of the bank statements immediately preceding and including the date of death must be filed. [LRSF 14.26 A.] (4) If the decedent was survived by parents (or siblings if the parents predeceased), the parents (or siblings) missing from item 9 of the petition must be listed in a verified supplement to the petition and must receive notice. (5) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/3/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
